title: Writing - Part 1: The Ageing Population
description: Your class has been discussing the social and economic challenges posed by an ageing population in many countries.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the challenges in your notes. You should explain which challenge you consider most pressing, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: An older population means more people requiring medical care, putting enormous strain on public health services.
As fewer working-age people support more retirees, governments struggle to fund adequate pensions.
Many elderly people live alone and have limited social contact, which seriously affects their physical and mental health.

structure.introduction: Introduce the demographic shift towards older populations and its implications.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first challenge and how it affects society.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second challenge and argue which requires more urgent attention.
structure.conclusion: Summarise your analysis and suggest a priority for policymakers.

content.modelAnswer:
Many developed countries are experiencing a demographic shift, with ageing populations creating significant social and economic pressures. Two of the most urgent challenges are the strain on healthcare systems and the growing problem of pension funding.

As the proportion of elderly people increases, demand for medical care rises accordingly. Hospitals, care homes and specialist services face overwhelming pressure, and governments must invest heavily in training healthcare workers and expanding facilities. Without this investment, waiting times will lengthen and quality of care will decline for everyone.

Pension sustainability is equally critical. When fewer working-age people are contributing taxes to support a growing number of retirees, pension systems risk becoming financially unviable. Governments may be forced to raise retirement ages or cut benefits, creating hardship for those who have worked hard their entire lives.

In conclusion, both challenges are serious, but I believe pension funding requires more urgent policy attention. A collapsed pension system would leave millions of elderly people without adequate income, creating widespread poverty among one of the most vulnerable groups in society.
